reasoning: The base rate for 'Strategic developments' in the British Indian Ocean Territory (BIOT) is exceptionally low, with only 1 event recorded over the past 360 days. To meet the resolution criteria, there would need to be 11 or more strategic developments within a 30-day window. BIOT is an isolated and highly restricted military facility with no civilian population (the recently stranded Tamil asylum seekers have been sent to the UK). The recent diplomatic agreement transferring sovereignty of the wider Chagos Archipelago to Mauritius was signed in May 2025. While Mauritius could theoretically begin administrative deployments to the outer islands, recording 11+ distinct 'Strategic development' events in a single month would require a sustained series of highly unusual occurrences—such as daily military manoeuvres, multiple discrete arrests, or persistent separate territory transfers. Given the lack of any news foreshadowing such a dense cluster of events leading up to the September resolution dates, the probability is rock-bottom. Uncertainty naturally increases for the more distant resolution dates (November 2025 and February 2026) due to the possibility of minor unforeseen developments as Mauritius takes over, but achieving 11 events in 30 days remains highly improbable.
